repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem:

Cannot remove a `@conceal` highlight when defined in a `highlights.scm`
file.

Solution:

Support a `@noconceal` highlight that works similarly to `@nospell` where it
overrides the conceal set on the range to remove it. Additionally, can
set the conceal metadata field to `\f` for the same behavior.

closes #35085

comment_data.body: Need to error out if the type is neither string nor boolean

this logic looks suspicious. what is the purpose of the `metadata.conceal ~= nil` check, since if `metadata.conceal=false` it will fallthrough to the latter part anyway? also, use parens for clarity, since `a and b or c and d` is not obvious.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -410,14 +410,28 @@ local function on_line_impl(self, win, buf, line, on_spell, on_conceal)
 
             local spell, spell_pri_offset = get_spell(capture_name)
 
+            local is_noconceal = capture_name == 'noconceal'
+            -- The "conceal" attribute can be set at the pattern level or on a particular capture
+            local conceal_attr = metadata.conceal ~= nil and metadata.conceal
+              or metadata[capture] and metadata[capture].conceal

comment_data.body:
yea that's it, i can also just create an if statement so that it's abundantly clear:

```lua
local conceal_attr = metadata.conceal
if conceal_attr == nil then
  conceal_attr = metadata[capture] and metadata[capture].conceal
end
```

comment_data.body: can this logic be simplified? using a ternary avoids the need for `---@type`.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -410,14 +410,31 @@ local function on_line_impl(self, win, buf, line, on_spell, on_conceal)
 
             local spell, spell_pri_offset = get_spell(capture_name)
 
+            local is_noconceal = capture_name == 'noconceal'
+            -- The "conceal" attribute can be set at the pattern level or on a particular capture
+            local conceal_attr = metadata.conceal
+            if conceal_attr == nil then
+              conceal_attr = metadata[capture] and metadata[capture].conceal
+            end
+            local conceal ---@type boolean|string?
+            if is_noconceal then
+              conceal = false
+            else
+              conceal = conceal_attr

comment_data.body: please, use ternaries for simple cases. verbose code is a cost.

since we are changing this, let's tighten up the wording
```suggestion
        VALIDATE(hl.conceal_char && vim_isprintc(ch), "%s", "conceal char must be printable", {
```
do this check before the logic above, and avoid the nesting.

Could we use an unprintable character to mean "clear"? Maybe ^L. That simplifies the logic, the docs, and avoids expanding the type. It's quirky, but this feature is niche so could make sense. @bfredl @clason @zeertzjq WDYT

Most of the logic in this PR is just dealing with the potential boolean.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -3049,11 +3049,15 @@ nvim_buf_set_extmark({buffer}, {ns_id}, {line}, {col}, {opts})
                   • cursorline_hl_group: highlight group used for the sign
                     column text when the cursor is on the same line as the
                     mark and 'cursorline' is enabled.
-                  • conceal: string which should be either empty or a single
-                    character. Enable concealing similar to |:syn-conceal|.
-                    When a character is supplied it is used as |:syn-cchar|.
-                    "hl_group" is used as highlight for the cchar if provided,
-                    otherwise it defaults to |hl-Conceal|.
+                  • conceal: either a boolean or a string.
+                    • when a string is given, it should be either empty or a

comment_data.body:
@justinmk I don't think this is a niche feature -- ever since we added conceal support, people have been asking for a way to disable _specific_ conceals. Right now, this either requires copying and modifying the bundled queries, or low-level and frankly hacky `disable_pattern()` calls. This PR could solve that issue nicely by just allowing a single targeted `@noconceal` pattern in a user query.

So I would say the logic needed for introducing this boolean is worth it.

@@ -479,7 +479,8 @@ local function styletable_treesitter(state)
       local c = q._query.captures[capture]
       if c ~= nil then
         local hlid = register_hl(state, '@' .. c .. '.' .. tree:lang())
-        if metadata.conceal and state.opt.conceallevel ~= 0 then
+        -- Form feed (\f) is used to clear conceal, so skip it
+        if metadata.conceal and metadata.conceal ~= '\f' and state.opt.conceallevel ~= 0 then

comment_data.body: should we skip the case above (which also sets "conceal")? just a micro-optimization i guess, but also clarifies intent.

yea, it's weird, which is why I felt like introducing the boolean made type things more explicit at least, but maybe at a cost of potentially forcing them to support something that they don't care about supporting

comment_data.body:
```suggestion
with |treesitter-directive-set!|.
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -522,6 +522,14 @@ character will be used: >query
     ; identifiers will be concealed with 'f'.
     ((identifier) @conceal (#set! conceal "foo"))
 <
+To remove a conceal highlight, you can use the `@noconceal` capture to
+override any existing conceal highlight set for the range. >query
+
+    ((identifier) @noconceal)
+<
+
+You can also do this by setting the `conceal` metadata to "\f" (form feed)
+with |vim.treesitter.query.add_directive()|.

comment_data.body: I wouldn't even mention this here; this is just going to confuse people who just want to disable that annoying markdown conceal.
comment_data.diff_hunk:

+You can also do this by setting the `conceal` metadata to "\f" (form feed)
+with |vim.treesitter.query.add_directive()|.

comment_data.body:
wait, we still need special handling here, just like [with the `\f` special case.](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/commit/7e402fed550ef6ce2dc66971d8ceae72ef282458#diff-bccef3ce45c389751e82b75d667a99ac5c0413dcf3d08a30f891bda5be131935)

how is this better? I'm genuinely asking. 

The code in `runtime/lua/vim/treesitter/highlighter.lua` is also more complex, and the entire rest of the PR has more changes.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -480,7 +480,8 @@ local function styletable_treesitter(state)
       if c ~= nil then
         local hlid = register_hl(state, '@' .. c .. '.' .. tree:lang())
         if metadata.conceal and state.opt.conceallevel ~= 0 then
-          styletable_insert_conceal(state, srow + 1, scol + 1, erow + 1, ecol + 1, metadata.conceal)
+          local conceal = metadata.conceal == true and '' or metadata.conceal --[[@as string]]
+          styletable_insert_conceal(state, srow + 1, scol + 1, erow + 1, ecol + 1, conceal)

comment_data.body: it's to be backwards compatible with existing "does this line have conceal" check by just checking `if metadata.conceal` instead of requiring `if metadata.conceal ~= '\f'` as well. if we want to completely remove special handling here, we can change the type to `false|string` and remove support for `conceal = true`

+          styletable_insert_conceal(state, srow + 1, scol + 1, erow + 1, ecol + 1, conceal)

comment_data.body: also with this approach, it would be caught with typechecking rather than silently failing

comment_regular_data.body: would appreciate some guidance on what types of tests would be appropriate for this change, if this change would be accepted at all 🙏

~potential problem/todo, getting an error (while override still works):~ fixed, was trying to edit [this line](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/fccd016a0fba58dc4149aa046c00e05d9fd25a95/runtime/lua/vim/treesitter/highlighter.lua#L438) to have the same logic for conceal as we do for spell, but seems like it doesn't apply so just left it the same.
```
Decoration provider "conceal_line" (ns=nvim.treesitter.highlighter):
Lua: ...ojects/neovim/runtime/lua/vim/treesitter/highlighter.lua:352: cannot set emphemeral mark outside of a decoration provider
stack traceback:
	[C]: in function 'nvim_buf_set_extmark'
	...ojects/neovim/runtime/lua/vim/treesitter/highlighter.lua:352: in function 'add_mark'
	...ojects/neovim/runtime/lua/vim/treesitter/highlighter.lua:455: in function 'fn'
	...ojects/neovim/runtime/lua/vim/treesitter/highlighter.lua:245: in function 'for_each_highlight_state'
	...ojects/neovim/runtime/lua/vim/treesitter/highlighter.lua:364: in function 'on_line_impl'
	...ojects/neovim/runtime/lua/vim/treesitter/highlighter.lua:529: in function <...ojects/neovim/runtime/lua/vim/treesitter/highlighter.lua:519>
```
@justinmk rebased and tested this again, would apreciate another look 🙏

I find the choice of `\f` instead of a boolean weird and hard to reason about. But I only care about the treesitter query interface, so if `@noconceal` works as expected (i.e., no messing with lower-level metadata messing is required), that's good enough for me.

yeah https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/35087#discussion_r3113852491 is maybe hinting that the special `\f` approach may be surprising and cause bugs. if the `conceal: boolean|string` approach avoids changes like that, then let's go with that. thanks for entertaining my suggestion :>
@justinmk @clason changed back to boolean approach, ready for another look!
oops broke ci, but just fixed it

title: feat(treesitter/extmark): support removing a conceal highlight
